tasks.subject: Task 1: Enable NuGet lockfiles repo-wide
tasks.description:
**Goal:** Add `RestorePackagesWithLockFile=true` to the root `Directory.Build.props`, run `dotnet restore` to generate per-project `packages.lock.json` files, and commit them. This makes restores reproducible (so the release workflow can use `--locked-mode`) and gives CI a stable cache key.

**Files:**
- Modify: `Directory.Build.props` (add one property)
- Create (via `dotnet restore`): `src/JoakimAnder.Toolbox/packages.lock.json`
- Create (via `dotnet restore`): `src/JoakimAnder.Toolbox.SourceGenerators/packages.lock.json`
- Create (via `dotnet restore`): `tests/JoakimAnder.Toolbox.Tests/packages.lock.json`
- Create (via `dotnet restore`): `tests/JoakimAnder.Toolbox.SourceGenerators.Tests/packages.lock.json`
- Create (via `dotnet restore`): `examples/JoakimAnder.Toolbox.Examples/packages.lock.json`

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] `Directory.Build.props` contains `<RestorePackagesWithLockFile>true</RestorePackagesWithLockFile>` inside the existing `<PropertyGroup>`.
- [ ] Exactly five `packages.lock.json` files exist, one per project.
- [ ] `dotnet restore --locked-mode` exits 0.
- [ ] `dotnet build -c Release` still passes with zero warnings.
- [ ] `dotnet test -c Release` still passes.

**Verify:** `dotnet restore --locked-mode && find . -name packages.lock.json -not -path './*/bin/*' -not -path './*/obj/*' | wc -l` â†’ exit 0 and count is `5`.

**Steps:** See plan task 1 in `docs/superpowers/plans/2026-05-23-cicd.md`.

```json:metadata
{"files": ["Directory.Build.props", "src/JoakimAnder.Toolbox/packages.lock.json", "src/JoakimAnder.Toolbox.SourceGenerators/packages.lock.json", "tests/JoakimAnder.Toolbox.Tests/packages.lock.json", "tests/JoakimAnder.Toolbox.SourceGenerators.Tests/packages.lock.json", "examples/JoakimAnder.Toolbox.Examples/packages.lock.json"], "verifyCommand": "dotnet restore --locked-mode && find . -name packages.lock.json -not -path './*/bin/*' -not -path './*/obj/*' | wc -l", "acceptanceCriteria": ["RestorePackagesWithLockFile=true added to root Directory.Build.props", "Five packages.lock.json files exist and are tracked", "dotnet restore --locked-mode exits 0", "dotnet build -c Release still passes with zero warnings", "dotnet test -c Release still passes"]}
```

tasks.subject: Task 2: CI workflow (build + test on push/PR)
tasks.description:
**Goal:** Create `.github/workflows/ci.yml` that builds and tests the solution on every push to `main` and every pull request targeting `main`.

**Files:**
- Create: `.github/workflows/ci.yml`

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] `.github/workflows/ci.yml` exists with the exact content from plan task 2.
- [ ] YAML is syntactically valid.
- [ ] `actions/checkout@v4` with `fetch-depth: 0` (MinVer).
- [ ] `actions/setup-dotnet@v4` reads `global.json` with cache keyed on `**/packages.lock.json`.
- [ ] Concurrency cancels in-progress for PRs only.
- [ ] `permissions: contents: read`.

**Verify:** `python3 -c "import yaml,sys; yaml.safe_load(open('.github/workflows/ci.yml')); print('ok')"` â†’ `ok`.

**Steps:** See plan task 2 in `docs/superpowers/plans/2026-05-23-cicd.md`.

```json:metadata
{"files": [".github/workflows/ci.yml"], "verifyCommand": "python3 -c \"import yaml,sys; yaml.safe_load(open('.github/workflows/ci.yml')); print('ok')\"", "acceptanceCriteria": [".github/workflows/ci.yml created with exact content from plan task 2", "YAML is syntactically valid", "fetch-depth: 0 set for MinVer", "actions/setup-dotnet@v4 reads global.json with cache keyed on packages.lock.json", "concurrency cancels in-progress for PRs only", "permissions limited to contents: read"]}
```

tasks.subject: Task 3: Release workflow (publish on tag push)
tasks.description:
**Goal:** Create `.github/workflows/release.yml` that fires on `v*` tag push, re-runs build + tests under `--locked-mode`, packs the main lib, exchanges OIDC for a NuGet API key, and pushes the `.nupkg` (which uploads the sibling `.snupkg` automatically).

**Files:**
- Create: `.github/workflows/release.yml`

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] `.github/workflows/release.yml` exists with the exact content from plan task 3.
- [ ] YAML is syntactically valid.
- [ ] Trigger is `push.tags: ['v*']` only.
- [ ] `permissions: id-token: write` and `contents: read`.
- [ ] Job uses `nuget-release` environment.
- [ ] `dotnet restore --locked-mode`.
- [ ] `dotnet pack --no-build`.
- [ ] `dotnet nuget push` uses `*.nupkg` glob and `--skip-duplicate`.

**Verify:** `python3 -c "import yaml,sys; yaml.safe_load(open('.github/workflows/release.yml')); print('ok')"` â†’ `ok`.

**Steps:** See plan task 3 in `docs/superpowers/plans/2026-05-23-cicd.md`.

```json:metadata
{"files": [".github/workflows/release.yml"], "verifyCommand": "python3 -c \"import yaml,sys; yaml.safe_load(open('.github/workflows/release.yml')); print('ok')\"", "acceptanceCriteria": [".github/workflows/release.yml created with exact content from plan task 3", "YAML is syntactically valid", "Trigger is push.tags v* only", "permissions includes id-token: write for OIDC", "Job uses nuget-release environment", "dotnet restore uses --locked-mode", "dotnet pack uses --no-build", "Push uses --skip-duplicate"]}
```

tasks.subject: Task 4: README CI badge and CHANGELOG entry
tasks.description:
**Goal:** Add a CI status badge to `README.md` (between the existing NuGet and License badges) and record the CI/CD wiring under `## [Unreleased] â†’ Added` in `CHANGELOG.md`.

**Files:**
- Modify: `README.md`
- Modify: `CHANGELOG.md`

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] `README.md` badge block reads NuGet â†’ CI â†’ License in order.
- [ ] CI badge URL is `https://github.com/JoakimAnder/Toolbox/actions/workflows/ci.yml/badge.svg?branch=main`.
- [ ] `CHANGELOG.md` `[Unreleased] â†’ Added` has the three CI/CD bullets.
- [ ] Existing CHANGELOG entries preserved.
- [ ] No other content changed.

**Verify:** `grep -c "actions/workflows/ci.yml/badge.svg" README.md` â†’ `1`, `grep -c "GitHub Actions CI workflow" CHANGELOG.md` â†’ `1`.

**Steps:** See plan task 4 in `docs/superpowers/plans/2026-05-23-cicd.md`.

```json:metadata
{"files": ["README.md", "CHANGELOG.md"], "verifyCommand": "grep -c \"actions/workflows/ci.yml/badge.svg\" README.md && grep -c \"GitHub Actions CI workflow\" CHANGELOG.md", "acceptanceCriteria": ["README.md badge block reads NuGet, CI, License in order", "CI badge URL points to actions/workflows/ci.yml with branch=main", "CHANGELOG [Unreleased] Added section gains three CI/CD bullets", "Existing CHANGELOG entries preserved"]}
```

tasks.subject: Task 5: End-to-end verification â€” push, manual setup, tag v0.1.0, observe publish
tasks.description:
**Goal:** Drive the full pipeline end-to-end on the real GitHub repository and the real nuget.org account. Push the committed work, complete the one-time manual setup (GitHub Deployment Environment + nuget.org trusted publisher), tag `v0.1.0`, and confirm: CI goes green, release workflow goes green, and the package becomes visible on nuget.org. This is the only acceptable proof that the CI/CD sub-project is done.

**USER-ORDERED GATE â€” NON-SKIPPABLE.** This task was requested by the user in the current conversation. It MUST NOT be closed by walking around it, by declaring it 'verified inline', or by substituting a cheaper check. Close only after every item in `acceptanceCriteria` has been re-validated independently, with output captured.

**This task requires actions on GitHub.com and nuget.org that an agent cannot perform autonomously.** A subagent assigned this task must hand off to the user with the exact steps in the plan.

**Files:** None modified. External state changes only.

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] Local `main` pushed to `origin/main` with commits from Tasks 1â€“4.
- [ ] `ci.yml` triggers and goes green on the push.
- [ ] `nuget-release` GitHub Deployment Environment exists (empty).
- [ ] Trusted-publisher policy on nuget.org: owner `JoakimAnder`, repo `Toolbox`, workflow `release.yml`, environment `nuget-release`, pattern `JoakimAnder.Toolbox`.
- [ ] `v0.1.0` annotated tag created and pushed.
- [ ] `release.yml` triggers and goes green (restore/build/test/pack/login/push).
- [ ] `https://www.nuget.org/packages/JoakimAnder.Toolbox/0.1.0` returns 200 with version 0.1.0 and README.
- [ ] `.snupkg` uploaded per `dotnet nuget push` step output.
- [ ] README CI badge renders green on GitHub.

**Verify:** Each criterion has an observable on GitHub or nuget.org. Capture URLs of green CI run, green release run, and published package page; post as close evidence.

**Steps:** See plan task 5 in `docs/superpowers/plans/2026-05-23-cicd.md` for the exact 8 user-driven steps.

```json:metadata
{"files": [], "verifyCommand": "echo 'Manual verification â€” see plan Step 8 for required close evidence (CI run URL, release run URL, nuget.org package URL, badge confirmation).'", "acceptanceCriteria": ["Local main pushed to origin", "CI workflow green on the push", "nuget-release GitHub Deployment Environment exists", "Trusted-publisher policy registered on nuget.org for JoakimAnder/Toolbox release.yml nuget-release", "v0.1.0 tag created and pushed", "Release workflow green on the tag", "Package visible at https://www.nuget.org/packages/JoakimAnder.Toolbox/0.1.0", "snupkg uploaded alongside per workflow output", "README CI badge renders green"], "userGate": true, "tags": ["user-gate"], "gateScope": "end-to-end CI/CD pipeline including nuget.org publish"}
```
